Progress Report On The Deposit Of Money Into A Blocked Account For The Minor, Aaliyah Howard
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Continued To Nov-05-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action Of Minor
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of the Minor, Gabriel Kutner.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Thuy Le, Esq. appeared with petitioner Jessica Valle, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Aaliyah Howard, who was not present.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Both Ms. Le and Ms. Valle appeared via CourtCall.
Order appointing Ms. Valle Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed September 13, 2018.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the Compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $100,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $25,000.00.
Medical Bills/Liens - $6,808.00.
Costs - $4,559.00.

The net balance of $63,633.00 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America - Financial Center, located at 500 Battery Street, San Francisco, CA 94111. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of the Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to September 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for a Progress Report on the Deposit of Money into a Blocked Account.

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PRESBYTERY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint is Granted. No opposition filed.

Counsel for the movant is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.
